mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that reduces discount rates, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns. The signal is always active because the direction of the rate change is continuously evaluated.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical easing cycles often precede equity rallies
failure_condition: If the rolling 12-month correlation between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (Rank IC < -0.05), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0%, the labor market slack depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical recessions are associated with UNRATE above 6%
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE > 6%, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad consumer and investment demand strength, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series are leading indicators of economic activity
failure_condition: If the composite signal (product of 3-month growth rates of RSAFS and HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year walk-forward window, or the interaction term is not significant in a multivariate regression (p > 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) exceeds 105, the economy is in an expansion regime with high capacity utilization, and further production gains are likely to sustain corporate profits and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO above 105 coincides with past expansion peaks
failure_condition: If in periods with INDPRO > 105, the median 20-day SPX return is not positive (p > 0.10 under a one-sided sign test), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Taylor rule suggests tighter policy when both rates and inflation rise
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS 1-month change multiplied by CPIAUCSL 1-month change) does not have a significant negative coefficient in predicting 5-day returns (p < 0.05), reject.

mechanism_narrative: A steady month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, which drives economic growth and corporate revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumption is a key component of GDP
failure_condition: If the 3-month average growth rate of RSAFS does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC >= 0.05 in full-sample test), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal labor market contraction and impending economic weakness, which depresses earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decline for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll declines are associated with recession onset
failure_condition: If after two consecutive monthly declines in PAYEMS, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 6%), a decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exacerbates economic weakness and signals further earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Output declines are more damaging when labor market is already weak
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE > 6%, the correlation between INDPRO 1-month change and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or the mean return is not negative, reject.
